evaluators.details: manual QA: none (no live run detected)

1) API contract correctness (20/20): New HealthController in correct package, @GetMapping resolving to /api/health via @RequestMapping("/api"), returns {"status":"UP"} with HTTP 200 via ResponseEntity.ok, matching CORS/style conventions seen in RatingController. Full credit. 2) Implementation quality (10/10): Clean, idiomatic, no smells — concise use of Map.of and ResponseEntity. 3) Discovery & design (7/10): Agent did explore existing code (found Application.java, listed Controllers, read RatingController for style) before writing, so not blind-wrote; however no explicit design statement/consideration beyond mirroring an existing controller — partial credit. 4) Specs & plan (0/10): No tech-spec or implementation-plan artifacts were produced. 5. Code review evidence (0/10): No self- or reviewer code review step occurred; agent went straight from write to summary. 6. Automated test (0/15): No JUnit/MockMvc test file was written — score is 0 per rubric. 7. Manual QA by AI (0/15): No evidence the agent ran the service or curled the endpoint; only static file creation happened. 8. Documentation quality (2.5/5): No new docs were created/updated, but no inaccurate docs were introduced either; the terse final chat summary is accurate but not a docs artifact — partial/minimal credit. 9. Scope discipline (5/5): Change is fully scoped — only the new controller file was added, no edits to pom.xml, application.properties, or unrelated files. Validation approach: NONE — the agent neither wrote an automated test nor performed manual/live QA of the endpoint. Weighted total ≈ 20+10+7+0+0+0+0+2.5+5 = 38.5, rounded to 38.
